query: 2026 Florida Republican gubernatorial primary status: Byron Donalds candidacy, opponents, Trump endorsement, polling, competitors dropping out. Who is running against Donalds and what are the polls showing as of mid-2026?
answer:
I've completed the research on the 2026 Florida Republican gubernatorial primary. Here's a summary of what I found as of mid-July 2026:

**Frontrunner:** U.S. Rep. Byron Donalds is the dominant frontrunner with Trump's early endorsement, leading independent public polls by 40+ points and posting record fundraising ($22M in Q1, $81M+ cumulative).

**Who's running against Donalds:**
- **Jay Collins** (Lt. Governor) — the main challenger, running a heavy ad blitz
- **James Fishback** (investment CEO) — facing an eligibility/residency legal challenge (July 21 hearing)
- **Paul Renner** (former House Speaker) — marginal, low single digits

**Casey DeSantis** never formally announced and appears only in hypothetical polls (where Donalds still leads big).

**Dropouts:** None formally, though rivals are "refusing to clear the field."

**Key July development:** DeSantis's own allies defected to Donalds — AG Uthmeier, CFO Ingoglia, Ag Commissioner Simpson, Senate President Albritton, and incoming legislative leaders all endorsed him. Ron DeSantis himself remains neutral.

**Polling snapshot:**
- AIF (June 3–7): Donalds 54%, Fishback 8%, Collins 5%, Renner 2%
- Emerson (April): Donalds 46%, Collins/Fishback 4% each
- Average (~270toWin): Donalds ~51%
- **Outlier** — Collins's own internal poll: Donalds 37%, Collins 20% (used to claim a tightening two-man race)

**General election note:** One May Change Research poll showed Democrat David Jolly narrowly ahead of Donalds 46%–42%.

Full prose report, structured data points, and all sources have been recorded.

key_facts: Florida 2026 primary is August 18, 2026; general election November 3, 2026; DeSantis is term-limited.
Byron Donalds (R-Naples, U.S. Rep FL-19) is the clear frontrunner with Trump's early endorsement.
Declared GOP candidates: Byron Donalds, Lt. Gov. Jay Collins, James Fishback (investment CEO), and former House Speaker Paul Renner.
Casey DeSantis never formally announced a candidacy as of mid-July 2026; appears only in hypothetical poll matchups.
No major declared candidate has formally dropped out; rivals are 'refusing to clear the field' for Donalds.

In early July 2026, DeSantis allies endorsed Donalds: AG James Uthmeier (~July 2), CFO Blaise Ingoglia, Ag Commissioner Wilton Simpson, Senate President Ben Albritton, plus incoming House Speaker and Senate President.
Ron DeSantis himself has NOT endorsed anyone and remains neutral as of mid-July 2026.
AIF poll (June 3-7, 2026): Donalds 54%, Fishback 8%, Collins 5%, Renner 2%, undecided 31%.
Emerson College (~April 2026): Donalds 46%, Collins 4%, Fishback 4%, undecided 39%; with Casey DeSantis hypothetical, Donalds 44% vs DeSantis 7%.
270toWin polling average: Donalds ~51%, Fishback ~8.5%, Collins ~6.5%, Renner ~2.5%.
Collins internal poll (~early July 2026, David Jay Wolfson): Donalds 37%, Collins 20%, Fishback 9%, Renner 2%, undecided 28% - an outlier used to claim a tightening two-man race.
James Fishback faces an eligibility challenge over Florida's 7-year residency requirement; a two-day hearing was set to begin July 21, 2026.
Donalds posted record fundraising: ~$22M in Q1 2026, cumulative reported at $81M+ by mid-2026.
UNF poll found Donalds jumps to ~47% vs Casey DeSantis ~12% once voters learn of Trump's endorsement.
General election context: Change Research (May 13-16, 2026) showed Democrat David Jolly 46% vs Donalds 42%; Jolly leads Democratic primary by ~15 points.
